goal: drive qualified traffic to Codeberg first and GitHub second by keeping the directory-lane chooser truthful
why_this_action: The live distribution loop had already executed fresh directory submissions such as VB Web AI Tools, but channel discovery was still partially dependent on manual constants and could miss recent execution truth when choosing the next lane. That risks wasting future high-leverage slots on rediscovery, duplicate checking, or stale lane ranking instead of fresh Codeberg-first distribution.
shared_findings_used: agents/marketing/logs/channel_discovery.json: current working-lane source of truth for directory selection
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_workflow_audit_latest.json: current bottleneck is distribution and message-to-primary-repo conversion

summary: added vbwebtools as a first-class tracked easy-submit lane
taught channel discovery to seed results from verified marketing submission execution logs instead of relying only on hard-coded/manual discovery state
refreshed channel_discovery.json so vbwebtools is present in tried/results/working state for future lane selection
added a regression test for execution-log seeding

outcome.marketing_effect: future directory-lane selection is less likely to miss fresh proven lanes or rediscover them from scratch, which increases the odds that the next external marketing slot goes to a truly new Codeberg-first surface

next_review.success_signal: the next distribution planning pass sees vbwebtools among working lanes and incorporates execution-log truth without manual backfill
